description:
An Energy Storage Systems Engineer at a utility is evaluating a 10 MW / 40 MWh Lithium-ion NMC battery project. The manufacturer's warranty guarantees the battery will maintain at least 80% of its original capacity for 10 years, provided it cycles no more than once per day. Model this system over a 20-year analysis period using a Standalone Battery (Single Owner) financial model with both Calendar Life and Cycle Life degradation models enabled. Configure a manual dispatch schedule where the battery charges from the grid at maximum capacity from 00:00 to 04:00 and discharges at maximum capacity from 16:00 to 20:00 daily, resting during all other hours. 

Extract the annual State of Health (maximum capacity percentage) at the end of each year and identify the first year (1-20) where the State of Health drops below 80.0% (set to null if it never drops below 80%). Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/battery_degradation_report.json using the following structure:

{
  "project_name": "10MW_Daily_Cycle_Degradation",
  "analysis_period_years": 20,
  "chemistry": "Lithium-ion NMC",
  "yearly_state_of_health_pct": [20 float values],
  "year_crossing_80_pct": integer or null
}
